claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific configuration isolation and boundary enforcement to prevent drift between Claude, Codex, and custom provider assumptions in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: Provider contract normalization defines distinct capabilities per provider (Claude, Codex, custom) with separate sandbox modes, approval policies, and file write permissions.
evidence.why: This is the foundational contract that prevents provider assumption drift by explicitly declaring each provider's capabilities and constraints.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation generates provider-specific command-line arguments: Claude uses --permission-mode, --no-session-persistence, --max-budget-usd flags; Codex uses --sandbox, --cd, --output-last-message; Custom uses rendered command with -c shell execution.
evidence.why: Provider invocation is isolated at the command level, preventing cross-contamination of provider-specific configuration.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes provider settings: clears Claude settings/tools when provider is Codex (line 443); sets agent_tool_profile for Claude (line 457); sets custom_sandbox for custom providers (line 460).
evidence.why: Configuration isolation prevents provider-specific assumptions from leaking between providers during evidence dispatch.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot system uses git ls-files or filesystem walk to capture file digests before/after agent execution, detecting mutations outside allowed artifact roots.
evidence.why: Independent of provider implementation, this system detects any project file mutations regardless of which provider runs the evidence agent.

evidence.summary: Runtime execution flow: capture boundary snapshot, run evidence agents sequentially, capture post-execution snapshot, compare for violations, inject boundary violations into readiness blockers.
evidence.why: The enforcement mechanism runs provider-agnostically to detect boundary violations from any provider.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject and parseJsonCandidate implement JSON repair for common provider output variations: line range format (42-88), quoted keys with spaces, trailing commas, fenced code blocks.
evidence.why: Tolerates minor provider-specific JSON formatting differences while maintaining schema validation.

evidence.summary: sanitizeMissing filters out status phrases like 'no missing data' that would incorrectly populate the missing array, keeping it empty only when truly nothing is missing.
evidence.why: Prevents status text from being misinterpreted as missing evidence blockers across different provider outputs.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope and related functions normalize evidence scope declarations from provider outputs, mapping various field names (scope, evidence_scope, source_scope) to canonical values.
evidence.why: Standardizes scope validation across providers regardless of field naming conventions in agent output.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues enforces that project-scoped evidence must have a repo-relative path or file locator, blocking claims without concrete project locators.
evidence.why: Prevents external or user claims from being accepted as project facts regardless of provider source.

evidence.summary: Test 'codex dispatch does not inherit a claude default model' verifies provider isolation by asserting config.ai.provider is codex, codex_model is not claude-prefixed, and model field doesn't contain 'claude'.
evidence.why: Test evidence that provider configuration is properly isolated during dispatch.

evidence.summary: Test 'claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags' verifies Claude evidence agents receive empty settings/tools strings, relying on prompt contract and boundary enforcement instead of CLI flags.
evidence.why: Test evidence that Claude's safety model uses prompt-based boundaries rather than tool-restriction flags.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates an agent writing outside allowed roots and verifies status is blocked with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION error.
evidence.why: Test evidence that boundary enforcement works regardless of agent output quality.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'Claude provider dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags; agent_tool_profile only expresses semantic mode; real safety comes from prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots, and readiness blockers.'
evidence.why: Design documentation confirms the security model relies on boundary enforcement rather than provider-specific tool restrictions.

risks: If a provider silently ignores prompt instructions while retaining full tool capabilities, the boundary snapshot is the only remaining safety control.
JSON repair logic could incorrectly normalize malformed but semantically valid JSON from an unfamiliar provider.
Git-based boundary detection fails if the repo has uncommitted changes that are not part of the agent's actions.
missing: No evidence of Codex or custom provider being tested with real agent execution in a demand evidence context.
No evidence of stress testing the boundary system against agents that deliberately attempt to mutate project files.
No evidence of how the system handles provider timeout or crash scenarios during evidence dispatch.

result.notes: Demand evidence dispatch has strong provider isolation through configuration normalization, boundary enforcement, and JSON repair. The implementation matches the documented security model. The remaining risks are mitigated by the boundary snapshot system and readiness gate.
